nodes.x: The king’s heart is in the hand of the Lord, as the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.
Let the rivers and all the waters murmur harmonious anthems to their eternal king.

And the Lord shall utterly destroy the tongue of the Egyptian sea; and with his mighty wind he shall shake his hand over the river, and shall smite it in the seven streams, and make men go over dry shod.

Now therefore, behold, the Lord bringeth up upon them the waters of the river, strong and many, even the king of Assyria, and all his glory: and he shall come up over all his channels, and go over all his banks:

Now therefore, behold, the Lord bringeth up upon them the waters of the river, strong and many, even the king of Assyria and all his glory; and he shall come up over all his channels, and go over all his banks.

And the Lord shall utterly destroy the tongue of the Egyptian sea; and with his mighty wind shall he shake his hand over the river, and shall smite it in the seven streams, and make men go over dryshod.
Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the Lord pondereth the hearts.
For the ways of man are before the eyes of the Lord, and he pondereth all his goings.
A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.
For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;
To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the Lord than sacrifice.
To do justice and judgment is more acceptable tb the Lord than sacrifice.— Solomon.
For thou desirest not sacrifice; else would I give it: thou delightest not in burnt offering.
And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which he shall offer unto the Lord.
Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;
An high look, and a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked, is sin.
The labour of the righteous tendeth to life: the fruit of the wicked to sin.

This thought gives joy and delight to the reflecting mind, for, as has been observed, man possesses the germ of all the attributes and power that are possessed by God his heavenly Father. I wish you to understand that sin is not an attribute in the nature of man, but it is an inversion of the attributes God has placed in him. Righteousness tends to an eternal duration of organized intelligence,…
Righteousness keepeth him that is upright in the way: but wickedness overthroweth the sinner.
In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin: but he that refraineth his lips is wise.

4. Woe unto you, ye sinners, for your riches make you appear like
       the righteous,
    But your hearts convict you of being sinners,
    And this fact shall be a testimony against you for a memorial of
       (your) evil deeds.
The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want.
Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:
Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a vanity tossed to and fro of them that seek death.
Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death.
Death and life are in the power of the tongue: and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.
Whiles they see vanity unto thee, whiles they divine a lie unto thee, to bring thee upon the necks of them that are slain, of the wicked, whose day is come, when their iniquity shall have an end.
For when they speak great swelling words of vanity, they allure through the lusts of the flesh, through much wantonness, those that were clean escaped from them who live in error.
Which long for death, but it cometh not; and dig for it more than for hid treasures;
The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to do judgment.
But, behold, the judgments of God will overtake the wicked; and it is by the wicked that the wicked are punished; for it is the wicked that stir up the hearts of the children of men unto bloodshed.
Let death seize upon them, and let them go down quick into hell: for wickedness is in their dwellings, and among them.
The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.
Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquity?
The way of man is froward and strange: but as for the pure, his work is right.
Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?

man who grows up without sin from the purity of his youth, who lives and dies a fit temple for the abode of the Holy Spirit. A man may in an hour, in an unguarded moment say and do things that would affect him throughout the never-ending ages of eternity. We should, therefore, be the more careful of our course and conduct in life, and hold fast to that which is given unto us, and progress and go…

XL VII.  But  the  original  man,  he  who  was  created  out  of 
the  clay,  the  primeval  founder  of  all  our  race,  appears  to  me 
to  have  been  most  excellent  in  both  particulars,  in  both  soul 
and  body,  and  to  have  been  very  far  superior  to  all  the  men  of 
subsequent  ages  from  his  pre-eminent  excellence  in  both  parts. 
For  he  in  truth  was  really …
Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.
It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house.
It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman and in a wide house.
It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.
For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,
Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife.
Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:
The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.
Devise not evil against thy neighbour, seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.
He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.
The righteous is more excellent than his neighbour: but the way of the wicked seduceth them.
Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
When the scorner is punished, the simple is made wise: and when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.
A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth.
Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware: and reprove one that hath understanding, and he will understand knowledge.
If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it.
A scorner loveth not one that reproveth him: neither will he go unto the wise.
A wise son heareth his father’s instruction: but a scorner heareth not rebuke.
The righteous man wisely considereth the house of the wicked: but God overthroweth the wicked for their wickedness.
The wicked are overthrown, and are not: but the house of the righteous shall stand.
The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.
The house of the wicked shall be overthrown: but the tabernacle of the upright shall flourish.
In the house of the righteous is much treasure: but in the revenues of the wicked is trouble.
Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard.
He shall not cry, nor lift up, nor cause his voice to be heard in the street.
Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?
He shall not strive, nor cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.
So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.
This poor man cried, and the Lord heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.
A gift in secret pacifieth anger: and a reward in the bosom strong wrath.

beloved by their families. Anger should never be permitted to rise in our bosoms, and words suggested by angry feelings should never be permitted to pass our lips. “A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.” “Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous;” but “The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.” All that we possess…
Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:

In our daily pursuits in life, of whatever nature and kind, Latter-day
Saints, and especially those who hold important positions in the
Kingdom of God, should maintain a uniform and even temper, both
when at home and when abroad. They should not suffer reverses and
unpleasant circumstances to sour their natures and render them fretful
and unsocial at home, speaking words full of bitterness and…
Be not hasty in thy spirit to be angry: for anger resteth in the bosom of fools.
It is joy to the just to do judgment: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.
The way of the Lord is strength to the upright: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.
When the wicked spring as the grass, and when all the workers of iniquity do flourish; it is that they shall be destroyed for ever:
For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing, whether it be good, or whether it be evil.

For behold, there is a curse upon all this land, that destruction shall come upon all those workers of darkness, according to the power of God, when they are fully ripe; therefore I desire that this people might not be destroyed.
The man that wandereth out of the way of understanding shall remain in the congregation of the dead.
Let the Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, set a man over the congregation,

And any man that shall go and preach this gospel of the kingdom, and fail not to continue faithful in all things, shall not be weary in mind, neither darkened, neither in body, limb, nor joint; and a hair of his head shall not fall to the ground unnoticed. And they shall not go hungry, neither athirst.
I the Lord have said, I will surely do it unto all this evil congregation, that are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.
And they who have slept in their graves shall come forth, for their graves shall be opened; and they also shall be caught up to meet him in the midst of the pillar of heaven—
He that loveth pleasure shall be a poor man: he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.
And wine that maketh glad the heart of man, and oil to make his face to shine, and bread which strengtheneth man’s heart.
He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall he prolong the perfection thereof upon the earth.
He delighteth not in the strength of the horse: he taketh not pleasure in the legs of a man.
He that oppresseth the poor to increase his riches, and he that giveth to the rich, shall surely come to want.
¶ Thus saith the Lord, Let not the wise man glory in his wisdom, neither let the mighty man glory in his might, let not the rich man glory in his riches:
The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.
The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him.
Take away the wicked from before the king, and his throne shall be established in righteousness.
Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end; but establish the just: for the righteous God trieth the hearts and reins.
The words of the wicked are to lie in wait for blood: but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.
Who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the frowardness of the wicked;
It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.
It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house.

"My animal nature got the upper hand, and I became, for the time being,
cruel.  My very passion, as is often the case under such conditions, was
an incentive rather than a check, to that which, in the language of
hypocrisy, is termed ’religious anger,’ but which is often little better
than the counter thrust of a wounded heart.  I will, I thought, use my
power against this girl, and make her see…
There is treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up.
For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, He taketh the wise in their own craftiness.
For there is no remembrance of the wise more than of the fool for ever; seeing that which now is in the days to come shall all be forgotten. And how dieth the wise man? as the fool.
For he seeth that wise men die, likewise the fool and the brutish person perish, and leave their wealth to others.
Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
And who knoweth whether he shall be a wise man or a fool? yet shall he have rule over all my labour wherein I have laboured, and wherein I have shewed myself wise under the sun. This is also vanity.
He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.
He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth in his heart.
As righteousness tendeth to life: so he that pursueth evil pursueth it to his own death.
Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
In holiness and righteousness before him, all the days of our life.
A wise man scaleth the city of the mighty, and casteth down the strength of the confidence thereof.
Wisdom strengtheneth the wise more than ten mighty men which are in the city.
A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.
¶ Woe to him that buildeth a town with blood, and stablisheth a city by iniquity!
Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.
He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction.
The mouth of the righteous speaketh wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment.
He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die.
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lips.
Proud and haughty scorner is his name, who dealeth in proud wrath.
Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud, and abase him.
For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one.
Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?
He that reproveth a scorner getteth to himself shame: and he that rebuketh a wicked man getteth himself a blot.
Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giveth grace unto the lowly.
The desire of the slothful killeth him; for his hands refuse to labour.
The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute.
And he shall snatch on the right hand and be hungry; and he shall eat on the left hand and they shall not be satisfied; they shall eat every man the flesh of his own arm—
And he shall snatch on the right hand, and be hungry; and he shall eat on the left hand, and they shall not be satisfied: they shall eat every man the flesh of his own arm:
But he that doeth not anything until he is commanded, and receiveth a commandment with doubtful heart, and keepeth it with slothfulness, the same is damned.
He coveteth greedily all the day long: but the righteous giveth and spareth not.
He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy.
For the wicked boasteth of his heart’s desire, and blesseth the covetous, whom the Lord abhorreth.
So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.
And he said unto them, Take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.
He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.
The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more, when he bringeth it with a wicked mind?
The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord: but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the Lord: but the words of the pure are pleasant words.
A false witness shall perish: but the man that heareth speaketh constantly.
A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall perish.
A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall not escape.
A false witness that speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.
He that speaketh truth sheweth forth righteousness: but a false witness deceit.
A true witness delivereth souls: but a deceitful witness speaketh lies.
A wicked man hardeneth his face: but as for the upright, he directeth his way.
The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul.
Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.
Let favour be shewed to the wicked, yet will he not learn righteousness: in the land of uprightness will he deal unjustly, and will not behold the majesty of the Lord.
¶ A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth.
There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the Lord.
I neither learned wisdom, nor have the knowledge of the holy.
How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?

Wherefore, brethren, seek not to counsel the Lord, but to take counsel from his hand. For behold, ye yourselves know that he counseleth in wisdom, and in justice, and in great mercy, over all his works.
The horse is prepared against the day of battle: but safety is of the Lord.
An horse is a vain thing for safety: neither shall he deliver any by his great strength.

¶ And they shall be as mighty men, which tread down their enemies in the mire of the streets in the battle: and they shall fight, because the Lord is with them, and the riders on horses shall be confounded.
Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?

When thou goest out to battle against thine enemies, and seest horses, and chariots, and a people more than thou, be not afraid of them: for the Lord thy God is with thee, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
